title: Write a Helm Deployment Template
description: Complete a Helm deployment template that uses Values, Chart metadata, and the fullname helper. The template should be production-quality.
starterCode:
# templates/deployment.yaml
# Complete this Helm chart deployment template
# Requirements:
# 1. Use {{ include "mychart.fullname" . }} for the deployment name
# 2. Set replicas from .Values.replicaCount
# 3. Use the chart labels helper: {{ include "mychart.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
# 4. Set image as {{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: # Add Helm fullname template here
  labels:
    # Add labels helper here
spec:
  replicas: # Get from values
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      {{- include "mychart.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        {{- include "mychart.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}
          image: # Set image from values with tag defaulting to AppVersion
          ports:
            - containerPort: {{ .Values.service.port }}
              protocol: TCP
hints: Use {{ include "mychart.fullname" . }} for the deployment name — never hardcode it
The | nindent 4 pipeline properly indents multi-line template output
Use | default .Chart.AppVersion as a fallback for the image tag

validationRules.errorMessage: Use {{ include "mychart.fullname" . }} for the deployment name
Include the labels helper with nindent

simulatedOutput.success:
helm template mychart ./mychart
---
# Source: mychart/templates/deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: myrelease-mychart
  labels:
    helm.sh/chart: mychart-0.1.0
    app.kubernetes.io/name: mychart
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: myrelease
    app.kubernetes.io/version: "1.0.0"
spec:
  replicas: 2
  ...

Template rendered successfully!
simulatedOutput.failure: Error: template rendering failed. Check that you use .Values references correctly and include the required helpers.
